summary: Soundboard recording of the NYE ’83 show captures an electrifying Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence, but occasional hiss and drop‑outs keep the audio from being flawless.
review: The performance is highlighted by a scorching Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World that many reviewers call the best they’ve ever heard, plus a memorable Good Night Irene arrangement and a sweet Brokedown Palace.  The soundboard source is generally clear and “crispy,” yet listeners note noticeable hiss and a few channel drop‑outs that require fixing.  Overall the show is enjoyable with several high points, though some songs feel flat and Jerry’s vocals were not at their peak.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical highlights (especially Estimated Prophet→Eyes) while noting only moderate recording flaws; the weighted average of show (≈4.2) and recording quality (≈3.5) yields a solid 4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced soundboard capture, but noticeable tape hiss and isolated drop‑outs (e.g., in Touch of Grey) that some reviewers had to patch.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical three‑set NYE layout with a solid first set, an unremarkable second set except for the Prophet/Eyes pairing, and a fun third set that includes surprise moments like Good Night Irene.  The encore was tacked onto the end of the second set, adding to the quirky flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals were thin or deteriorating on this night, but his guitar work—especially on Estimated Prophet—was virtuoso and carried the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Attempted to start Morning Dew in the third set before being sidetracked by Good Night Irene.
band_member_comments.Bob: Delivered a brief “Mighty Fine” comment after Good Night Irene; no major vocal critiques beyond the general note about his voice.
